content:
| Property          | Value                               | Notes                                                                     |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Density (g/cm³)   | 8.908                               | At 20°C                                                                   |
| Melting Point (°C) | 1455                                 |  Slight variations possible depending on purity                              |
| Young's Modulus (GPa) | 200                                  |  Can vary with processing and alloying.                                      |
| Thermal Conductivity (W/m·K) | 90                                   | At room temperature; decreases with increasing temperature.                  |
| Specific Heat (J/g·K) | 0.443                               | At room temperature.                                                       |
| Tensile Strength (MPa) | Varies greatly with processing (e.g., 400-700 MPa for annealed, higher for cold-worked) |  Highly dependent on metallurgical state.                                     |
| Laser Absorption | High at specific wavelengths (e.g., 1064 nm) |  Absorption depends strongly on wavelength and surface finish;  oxidized surfaces may exhibit different behavior.  |
| Ablation Threshold (J/cm²) | Varies with laser parameters and surface condition | Requires careful parameter optimization to avoid damage to underlying substrate.  |


Laser cleaning applications often involve removing surface contaminants from nickel components.  However, improper laser parameters can lead to undesirable surface modifications like melting or heat-affected zones.  Careful control of laser fluence and pulse duration is crucial.  Post-cleaning surface analysis is recommended to confirm successful removal of contaminants without damage.
